meta.description: LLM Agent Dev Tips — daily tips for SvelteKit + TypeScript developers

tips.title: Context Windows as Architecture
tips.subtitle: Feed Agents the Right Slice
tips.summary: An LLM agent is only as good as the context you hand it — structuring your SvelteKit codebase so that any feature's full picture fits in a single, coherent context window is the highest-leverage architectural decision you can make when working with coding agents.
tips.subject: DevTip #001 — Context Windows as Architecture

tips.title: Agent-First API Design
tips.subtitle: Types Are the Spec
tips.summary: A well-typed, self-documenting TypeScript API surface lets an LLM agent reason about, extend, and debug your routes without ever needing you to explain business logic — your code is the documentation.
key_concepts: Consistent REST to service function naming (1:1 mapping)
Zod schemas as single source of truth
JSDoc as machine-readable business logic spec
Auto-generate OpenAPI spec from Zod at build time
tips.subject: DevTip #002 — Agent-First API Design

tips.title: Prompt Chaining for UI Generation
tips.subtitle: Consistent Components Every Time
tips.summary: One-shot UI generation from LLM agents produces inconsistent, design-system-ignorant components — breaking the chain into discrete, primed steps produces output you can ship without a rewrite.
key_concepts: 4-step UI generation chain: prime, contract, generate, harden
Design tokens file as agent context primer
tips.subject: DevTip #003 — Prompt Chaining for UI Generation
